title: Urban Green Space Cooling Effect And Heat Mortality Reduction Potential
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping urban green-space distribution and estimating cooling effects and heat-mortality reduction potential in LMIC cities, with explicit satellite-to-ground temperature validation, green-space-type differentiation, and informal-settlement vulnerability documentation.
why_it_matters: Urban green spaces reduce local temperature by 1-5°C through shading and evapotranspiration, but nearly all evidence comes from high-income temperate cities. The compact, densely built, rapidly growing cities of South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, and the Middle East — where heat mortality risk is highest — have almost no systematic green-space cooling measurement. Informal settlements typically have 5-10 times less green space per capita than formal areas and surface temperatures 3-8°C higher. Without verified neighborhood-scale cooling analysis, municipal green-space investments cannot be targeted where they would most reduce heat exposure for the most vulnerable populations.

success_metrics.target: Every land-surface temperature claim documents the relationship between satellite LST and ground-level air temperature for the analyzed city, with explicit climate-zone and humidity caveats.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against LST-to-air-temperature validation methodology.

success_metrics.target: No cooling-effect claim is merged without documenting which green-space types (parks, street trees, green roofs, community gardens) are analyzed and how their cooling effectiveness varies by size, vegetation, and irrigation status.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms green-space-type classification methodology and irrigation-context documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every neighborhood-scale heat-exposure claim documents green-space availability and cooling capacity in informal settlements relative to formal residential areas.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms informal-settlement data availability and vulnerability-context documentation.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, temperature-validation safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
